mappings.evidence_note: Compulsory part: three strands (Number and Algebra; Measures, Shapes and Space; Data Handling) plus advanced learning units. Foundation and non-foundation topics distinguished.

Number and Algebra strand: quadratic equations, functions and graphs, exponential and logarithmic functions, polynomials, more equations, variation, sequences, inequalities and linear programming, more functions and graphs.
Learning unit 1: quadratic equations, discriminant, complex numbers.
Learning unit 2: functions, domain, co-domain, quadratic function graphs, max/min.
Learning unit 3: rational indices, laws of logarithms, exponential/logarithmic functions and equations.
Learning unit 4: polynomial division, remainder and factor theorems, HCF, LCM, rational functions.
Learning unit 5: simultaneous equations, equations reducible to quadratics, applications.
Learning unit 6: direct, inverse, joint and partial variation.
Learning unit 7: arithmetic and geometric sequences and summation, infinite geometric series.
Learning unit 8: linear and quadratic inequalities, linear programming.
Learning unit 9: graphs of various functions, transformations.
Measures, Shapes and Space strand: straight lines, properties of circles, locus, equations of circles, more trigonometry.
Learning unit 10: equations of straight lines, intersection of two lines.
Learning unit 11: chords, arcs, angles, cyclic quadrilaterals, tangents, circle proofs.
Learning unit 12: locus concepts, equations of loci.
Learning unit 13: equation of a circle, intersection of line and circle, tangent.
Learning unit 14: trigonometric functions and graphs, sine/cosine formulae, 3-D geometry.
Data Handling strand: permutations and combinations, more probability, measures of dispersion, uses and abuses of statistics.
Learning unit 15: addition and multiplication principles, permutations, combinations.
Learning unit 16: set notation, laws of probability, conditional probability.
Learning unit 17: range, inter-quartile range, box plots, standard deviation, standard scores.
Learning unit 18: sampling techniques, questionnaire design, uses and misuses of statistics.
Advanced learning units: advanced applications and exploration/research. Not standard public-exam content topics.
Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics): foundation knowledge, calculus, statistics, advanced learning unit.
Module 1 foundation: binomial expansion, exponential and logarithmic functions.
Module 1 learning unit 1: binomial expansion for positive integer index.
Module 1 learning unit 2: e, exponential/logarithmic functions, linearisation of non-linear relations.
Module 1 calculus: limits, derivatives, differentiation, second derivatives, applications, indefinite and definite integration, trapezoidal rule.
Module 1 statistics: conditional probability and Bayes' theorem, random variables, probability distributions, binomial, Poisson, normal, sampling distributions, confidence intervals.
Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus): foundation knowledge, algebra, calculus, advanced learning unit.
Module 2 foundation: odd/even functions, mathematical induction, binomial theorem, trigonometric functions, introduction to e.
Module 2 algebra: determinants, matrices, systems of linear equations, vectors, scalar and vector products, applications of vectors.
Module 2 calculus: limits, differentiation, second derivatives, applications, indefinite and definite integration, applications of definite integration.
Compulsory Paper 1: conventional questions, 65%, 2 hours 15 minutes.
Compulsory Paper 2: multiple-choice questions, 35%, 1 hour 15 minutes.
Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics) paper: conventional questions, 100%, 2 hours 30 minutes.
Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus) paper: conventional questions, 100%, 2 hours 30 minutes.
Assessment objectives: knowledge of mathematical content/concepts/techniques; familiar use of symbols; problem solving; mathematical communication.
Public exam uses multiple-choice questions, short questions and long questions.

draft_notes.note: No explicit difficulty taxonomy in source; inferred from question-style descriptions on p.93.
Advanced learning units (advanced applications / exploration and research) are curriculum components but are not standard public-exam content topics.
